---
title: "Modern HTML Reporting in R for Public Health"
subtitle: "Quarto + bslib + ggiraph workflows"
author: "Dylan Lewis"
format:
  revealjs:
    theme: [default, simple]
    slide-number: true
    toc: false
    transition: fade
    incremental: false
    chalkboard: false
    code-overflow: wrap
---

## Context: Reporting Constraints in PHS
- Shiny often blocked by hosting/governance.
- Need attractive, lightweight HTML outputs.
- Maintainability, reproducibility, and accessibility matter.

---

## Dashboards vs Parameterised Reports
- Not the same thing! <!-- pictures of layout differences -->
- When to choose a dashboard (explore / monitor live data).
- When to choose parameterised reports (batching per board/hospital).

---

## Quarto Overview <!-- (for Rmd newcomers) -->
- What Quarto is and why it replaced R Markdown.
- Same Markdown feel, modern features, long-term support.
- Reusable assets (themes, includes, metadata).

---

## Customising Quarto: YAML Essentials
- Key front‑matter options (`format`, `theme`, `toc`, `page-layout`).
- Title blocks, banners, and page metadata.
- Simple YAML changes that drastically improve look & feel.

---

## Customising Quarto: CSS / HTML Hooks
- Small CSS tweaks for identity/branding.
- HTML partials for headers/footers/notice blocks.
- Organising styles for reuse across reports.

---

## UI Components: Cards, Value Boxes, Accordions
- `bslib` package gives the “feel” of a Shiny app.
- Cards for repeated plots (in lieu of interactive inputs).
- Accordions for optional detail (in lieu of responsive text).

---

## Interactive Visuals: ggiraph (vs plotly)
- Why `ggiraph`?
  - Lightweight SVG output.
  - Easier to customise; fully readable markup.
  - R no longer officially supported for Plotly.
- When plotly is still useful <!-- zoom/pan heavy use‑cases -->
- Accessibility and load‑time considerations.

---

## Interactive Tables: `DT` + Extensions
- Search, filtering, and export buttons.
- Fixed headers and large-table handling.
- Data download options. <!-- mention downloadthis as alternative -->

---

## Breaking Free of Markdown
- Building HTML UIs with `bslib` / `htmltools` (no `.qmd`).
- When a pure-HTML layout beats document‑oriented Markdown.
- Trade‑offs: control vs authoring convenience.

---

## Saving Standalone HTML from R
- Exporting self‑contained HTML outputs.
- Packaging assets (CSS/JS/fonts) for offline sharing.
<!-- share my code for saving reports -->

---

## Recommended Templates & Starter Kits
- Quarto parameterised report template (bslib + ggiraph + DT).
- Pure HTML (`bslib` + `htmltools`) template.
